threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look shocked when a bus arrives late and you didn’t see anyone pull a ‘told you so’ face. It's all part of the charm.
Caught a late 333 last night; two lads were discussing a hidden rooftop bar near Coldharbour. Heard it's got a killer view of the skyline.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids building a mini skatepark out of old pallets in the Liselotte-Herrmann-Straße. That's pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a kebab shop open at 3 AM serving fresh döners while artists set up their galleries on the sidewalk. Pure Kreuzberg vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate BART delays without a second thought, while newcomers are still cursing their fate at the station.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with a surfboard on Muni, he casually asked the driver if he could ride in front. Only in SF do you have to negotiate surf etiquette on public transit.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm is in the little squares where locals sip café amb llet, hidden from the tourist rush.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their bewilderment at the lack of late-night transport - they’re still checking apps for buses at 2 AM.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Calling it the 'Overground' instead of the 'train' shows you’re fresh to the area. Locals know it's just different flavors of the same muddle.
Caught the N68 last night; a guy was arguing with the driver about how the stars align with bus timetables. Classic Brixton night, really.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I watched a guy in a tutu juggle flaming batons while waiting for the bus at Karl-Marx-Straße. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you enjoy a shawarma at 3 AM after a night out, then stumble into a stand-up gig in a hidden bar around the corner.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can name the BART delays by their train line like it’s a weather forecast. 'Expect red line chaos again tonight!'

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a lady singing opera on the 47 bus yesterday, people were crying. Only in SF can a commute turn into a concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but they miss the real charm in the tiny, tucked-away squares where locals sip vermut and chat.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still think a 'pà amb tomàquet' is just tomato on bread instead of an art form. You can spot them at the boqueria, asking for ketchup.
